  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To scope “clean this up,” which level of edit do you want: A) Copyedit only—grammar, concision, and phrasing without reordering or reframing (R1.6); B) Abstract-level rewrite—may change sentence order and tighten contribution framing (R1.6 + R1.5 + limited R1.1); or C) B plus auditing and tempering claims vs. reported numbers (adds R1.2/R1.3)? Please choose A, B, or C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The terse request to “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and grammar on the abstract.",
    "RX.1": "Because the scope of 'clean up' is ambiguous, uncertainties about the extent of edits should be explicitly surfaced.",
    "RX.2": "Polishing must avoid introducing stronger claims than the reported results support.",
    "RX.3": "The task should remain within the user-approved scope of edits rather than expanding into reframing or analysis without consent.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not create misleading implications about ASR evaluation practices or system performance.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable (e.g., tracked or clearly enumerated) so the author can verify and attribute edits to the source text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To scope “clean this up,” which level of edit do you want: A) Copyedit only—grammar, concision, and phrasing without reordering or reframing (R1.6); B) Abstract-level rewrite—may change sentence order and tighten contribution framing (R1.6 + R1.5 + limited R1.1); or C) B plus auditing and tempering claims vs. reported numbers (adds R1.2/R1.3)? Please choose A, B, or C.

rationale.R1.6: The terse request to “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and grammar on the abstract.
rationale.RX.1: Because the scope of 'clean up' is ambiguous, uncertainties about the extent of edits should be explicitly surfaced.
rationale.RX.2: Polishing must avoid introducing stronger claims than the reported results support.
rationale.RX.3: The task should remain within the user-approved scope of edits rather than expanding into reframing or analysis without consent.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not create misleading implications about ASR evaluation practices or system performance.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable (e.g., tracked or clearly enumerated) so the author can verify and attribute edits to the source text.
